Title: Keyboard Navigation - BOPIS Modal
Preconditions:
- BOPIS modal accessible
- Keyboard only (no mouse)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and find a product with BOPIS (pickup) option
[KEY] Tab to the pickup / Store Pickup option
[KEY] Press Enter to open the BOPIS modal
[KEY] Tab through all interactive elements in the modal
[ASSERT] Verify focus indicator is visible on each focused element
[KEY] Tab through store search input, location list, and map controls
[KEY] Press Enter to select a store location
[ACT] Complete the entire pickup flow using keyboard only (no mouse interaction)
Assertions:
[DOM] All interactive elements reachable via Tab key
[STATE] Visible focus indicator present on each focused element
[STATE] Logical tab order maintained throughout modal
[STATE] Entire BOPIS pickup flow completable without mouse
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no JS errors on Tab/Enter key events
Failure_Signals: focus indicator not visible on any element; keyboard trap outside modal; Tab skips an interactive element; flow not completable without mouse
Cleanup: Close modal if still open; clear cart

Title: Keyboard Navigation - Payment Form
Preconditions: - Payment form displayed (CyberSource or other processor)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and proceed to the payment step
[KEY] Tab into the card number field (may be inside a CyberSource iframe)
[KEY] Tab through fields in order: cardholder name → expiry date → CVV
[KEY] Tab to the Place Order / Submit button
[ASSERT] Verify focus transitions correctly into and out of iframe fields
[ACT] Enter test card data via keyboard only and submit the form
Assertions:
[DOM] Tab order: Card Number → Cardholder Name → Expiry → CVV → Submit
[STATE] Data enterable in every field via keyboard alone
[STATE] Focus transitions correctly into iframe and back to parent page
[STATE] Form submittable via keyboard without mouse
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no iframe focus errors; [NETWORK] payment tokenisation request fires on keyboard submit
Failure_Signals: focus stuck inside iframe; cannot reach CVV or submit via Tab; iframe focus handoff fails

Title: Focus Indicators - All Interactive
Preconditions: - Any page with interactive elements loaded
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} home page
[KEY] Tab through every interactive element on the page (buttons, inputs, links, custom components)
[ASSERT] Verify visible focus ring on buttons
[ASSERT] Verify visible focus ring on text inputs
[ASSERT] Verify visible focus ring on links
[ASSERT] Verify visible focus ring on custom components (dropdowns, pickers, toggles)
Assertions:
[DOM] Every interactive element has a visible focus indicator when focused
[STATE] Focus ring meets 3:1 contrast minimum against adjacent background
[STATE] High-contrast focus outline not removed via outline:none without replacement
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no CSS errors suppressing focus styles
Failure_Signals: focus indicator not visible on any interactive element; outline:none without visible replacement; focus ring contrast below 3:1

Title: Escape Key - Modal Close
Preconditions: - Modal open (BOPIS or other dialog)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a page on {{FRONT_URL}} with a modal trigger (e.g., BOPIS or filter dialog)
[ACT] Open the modal
[KEY] Press Escape key
[ASSERT] Verify modal closes
[ASSERT] Verify focus returns to the element that triggered the modal
Assertions:
[STATE] Escape key closes the open modal
[STATE] Focus returns to trigger element after modal close
[STATE] No residual keyboard trap after modal dismissal
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no JS errors on Escape keypress
Failure_Signals: modal does not close on Escape; focus does not return to trigger element; keyboard trap persists after close

Title: Focus Trap - Open Modal
Preconditions: - Modal open
Steps:
[NAV] Open a modal dialog on {{FRONT_URL}}
[KEY] Tab through all modal content to reach the last focusable element
[KEY] Press Tab once more past the last element
[ASSERT] Verify focus wraps back to the first focusable element inside the modal
[KEY] Press Shift+Tab from the first focusable element
[ASSERT] Verify focus wraps to the last focusable element inside the modal
Assertions:
[STATE] Focus is trapped within the open modal (intentional containment — correct per WCAG 2.1 exception)
[STATE] Tab cycles forward within modal only; cannot reach elements behind overlay
[STATE] Shift+Tab cycles backward within modal only
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no focus management errors
Failure_Signals: Tab exits modal and reaches background page elements; Shift+Tab escapes modal upward; focus not contained in modal

Title: Screen Reader - Modal Announcement
Preconditions:
- Screen reader active (NVDA on Windows or VoiceOver on macOS)
- BOPIS flow ready
Steps:
[NAV] Activate NVDA (Windows) or VoiceOver (macOS)
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and find a BOPIS-enabled product
[ACT] Trigger the pickup modal open
[WAIT] Wait for screen reader to announce the modal
[ASSERT] Listen for modal/dialog role announcement
[ASSERT] Verify modal title and purpose are stated by the screen reader
Assertions:
[STATE] Modal announced immediately on open
[STATE] Screen reader announces dialog title and purpose
[DOM] role="dialog" present on modal container; screen reader identifies it as Dialog
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no ARIA errors on modal open; [API] no silent failures loading store availability data
Failure_Signals: screen reader does not announce modal; role not announced; modal title absent from announcement
Cleanup: Close modal; deactivate screen reader if needed

Title: Screen Reader - List Items
Preconditions:
- Screen reader active
- Store location list visible in BOPIS modal
Steps:
[NAV] Open BOPIS modal on {{FRONT_URL}} with screen reader active
[NAV] Navigate to the store location list
[ASSERT] Listen for list item announcements from screen reader
[ASSERT] Verify list count is announced (e.g., "list of 5 items")
[KEY] Tab / arrow-key through items and confirm each item's details are read aloud
Assertions:
[STATE] Screen reader announces list items with position (e.g., "1 of 5")
[DOM] List uses semantic <ul>/<li> or role="listbox"/role="option" with correct count
[STATE] Each item's details (store name, distance, availability) are readable by screen reader
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no ARIA list warnings
Failure_Signals: screen reader does not announce list; count not stated; position not announced; item details unreadable

Title: Screen Reader - Form Fields
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Screen reader active
- Form open (checkout, registration, or search)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a form on {{FRONT_URL}} (e.g., checkout address form) with screen reader active
[KEY] Tab through each form field
[ASSERT] Verify field label is announced for each input
[ASSERT] Verify required status is announced for mandatory fields
[ACT] Submit form with errors and verify error messages are announced
Assertions:
[STATE] Field labels read aloud on field focus
[STATE] Required state announced (e.g., "required")
[STATE] Error messages announced when triggered
[DOM] <label for="id"> association correct or aria-labelledby set
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no missing-label warnings
Failure_Signals: field label not announced; required state silent; error message not announced after injection

Title: Screen Reader - Error Messages
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Screen reader active
- Form with client-side or server-side validation
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a form (e.g., checkout address or login) on {{FRONT_URL}} with screen reader active
[ACT] Submit the form leaving required fields empty to trigger validation errors
[ASSERT] Verify error messages are announced immediately by the screen reader
[KEY] Navigate to each errored field and confirm specific error text is read aloud
Assertions:
[STATE] Errors announced immediately upon form submission (via aria-live or focus shift)
[STATE] Can navigate to each error and hear its specific description
[STATE] Error text is clear and actionable
[DOM] Error messages linked to fields via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no ARIA live-region errors
Failure_Signals: errors not announced; screen reader silent after form submission; error text vague or missing

Title: Screen Reader - Buttons
Preconditions: - Screen reader active
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any page on {{FRONT_URL}} with interactive buttons using screen reader active
[KEY] Tab to each button
[ASSERT] Verify button text or aria-label is announced
[ASSERT] Verify "button" role is stated by screen reader
Assertions:
[STATE] Button accessible name (text or aria-label) announced on focus
[DOM] role="button" or native <button> element present
[STATE] Action purpose is clear from announcement — no generic "button" without context
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no missing-accessible-name warnings
Failure_Signals: button text not announced; role not stated; generic label with no context

Title: ARIA Labels - Custom Controls
Preconditions:
- Browser DevTools open
- Custom components visible (dropdowns, pickers, toggles)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a page on {{FRONT_URL}} with custom interactive components
[ACT] Open DevTools → Elements panel
[ACT] Inspect each custom control (dropdown, toggle, variant picker)
[ASSERT] Verify aria-label or aria-labelledby is present on each control
[ASSERT] Verify labels are descriptive and not generic
Assertions:
[DOM] aria-label or aria-labelledby present on every custom control
[DOM] Labels are descriptive (e.g., "Select size") not generic (e.g., "button")
[DOM] No interactive element with empty or missing accessible name
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe missing-label violations
Failure_Signals: ARIA label absent on custom control; generic label ("button", "click here"); aria-labelledby references non-existent id

Title: ARIA Roles - Modal Dialog
Preconditions:
- Modal open
- Browser DevTools open
Steps:
[NAV] Open a modal dialog on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Open DevTools → Elements panel
[ASSERT] Inspect modal container and verify role="dialog"
[ASSERT] Verify aria-modal="true" attribute is present
[ASSERT] Verify aria-labelledby references the id of the modal's visible title element
Assertions:
[DOM] role="dialog" set on modal container
[DOM] aria-modal="true" present
[DOM] aria-labelledby value matches the id of the modal's visible title
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no ARIA role warnings
Failure_Signals: role="dialog" absent; aria-modal missing; aria-labelledby references wrong or missing id

Title: ARIA Live Regions
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Dynamic content area visible (cart badge, notifications, search results)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} with a dynamic content area
[ACT] Trigger a content update (e.g., add item to cart, submit search query)
[ACT] Open DevTools → Elements and locate the aria-live region for that update
[ASSERT] Verify aria-live attribute is present with appropriate politeness level (polite or assertive)
[ASSERT] Verify the update is announced without being excessively verbose
Assertions:
[DOM] aria-live="polite" (or "assertive" for critical alerts) present on dynamic regions
[STATE] Content updates are announced to screen reader
[STATE] Announcement is concise and not repetitive
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no live-region-related ARIA errors
Failure_Signals: aria-live missing on dynamic region; update not announced; announcement excessively verbose or fires repeatedly

Title: axe DevTools Scan - Critical
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- axe DevTools browser extension installed
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to target page on {{FRONT_URL}} (home, catalog, cart, or checkout)
[ACT] Open the axe DevTools panel in the browser
[ACT] Run a full accessibility scan of the page
[ASSERT] Review the Critical violations list
[ACT] Document all critical findings with element selectors and WCAG criterion references
Assertions:
[STATE] Zero critical axe violations on the scanned page
[STATE] All critical issues resolved before release
[DOM] No elements blocking access to core functionality
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] axe.run() result: violations filtered by impact === 'critical' returns empty array; [NETWORK] page fully loaded before scan fires
Failure_Signals: axe reports one or more critical violations; core user journey blocked by accessibility barrier

Title: axe DevTools Scan - Serious
Preconditions: - axe DevTools browser extension installed
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to target page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Run axe DevTools scan
[ASSERT] Review Serious violations list
[ACT] Prioritise findings and document remediation plan with owner and timeline
Assertions:
[STATE] Serious issues documented with element selectors, WCAG criterion, and remediation owner
[STATE] Count of serious violations does not exceed established baseline (no regressions)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] axe.run() serious violation count does not exceed baseline
Failure_Signals: axe reports new serious violation not present in baseline; regression introduced since last scan

Title: Color Contrast - Text
Preconditions: - Contrast checker tool available (browser extension or DevTools accessibility panel)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Use contrast checker on body text (paragraphs, list items)
[ASSERT] Verify contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text
[ACT] Use contrast checker on headings and large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold)
[ASSERT] Verify contrast ratio ≥ 3:1 for large text
[ACT] Check link text contrast in default, hover, and visited states
Assertions:
[FORMAT] Normal text contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 against background
[FORMAT] Large text (≥18px or ≥14px bold) contrast ratio ≥ 3:1
[FORMAT] Link text contrast ≥ 4.5:1 in all states
[STATE] All text is readable against its background
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no CSS rendering errors; [NETWORK] fonts fully loaded
Failure_Signals: normal text contrast below 4.5:1; large text below 3:1; link text indistinguishable from background

Title: Color Contrast - UI Components
Preconditions: - Contrast checker tool available
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Check button background vs. button text contrast
[ASSERT] Verify ≥ 3:1 for button component
[ACT] Check input field border vs. page background contrast
[ASSERT] Verify ≥ 3:1 for input borders
[ACT] Check focus indicator contrast against its adjacent background
[ASSERT] Verify ≥ 3:1 for focus ring
Assertions:
[FORMAT] Buttons: contrast ratio ≥ 3:1 (text to background AND border to surrounding area)
[FORMAT] Input borders: ≥ 3:1 against page background
[FORMAT] Focus indicators: ≥ 3:1 against adjacent background
[STATE] Hover, active, and disabled interactive states all meet threshold
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no CSS theming errors
Failure_Signals: UI component contrast below 3:1; focus ring invisible against background; disabled state indistinguishable

Title: Color Not Sole Indicator
Preconditions:
- Error states visible on a form
- Status indicators visible (success, warning, info)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a form or status area on {{FRONT_URL}} and trigger an error state
[ASSERT] Verify error indication uses an icon or text label in addition to color
[NAV] Find success, warning, and info status indicators
[ASSERT] Verify each status uses a text label or icon independently of color
[ACT] Simulate colorblindness via DevTools Rendering → Emulate vision deficiencies and verify indicators remain distinguishable
Assertions:
[STATE] Errors conveyed by icon + color — not color alone
[STATE] Success/warning/info statuses have text label or icon independent of color
[STATE] All status indicators distinguishable in grayscale and colorblind simulation
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no rendering errors during vision emulation
Failure_Signals: error uses only red color with no icon or text; status indistinguishable in grayscale; colorblind simulation reveals loss of meaning

Title: Images - Alt Text
Preconditions: - Catalog or product detail page with images loaded
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to a catalog or product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}} [ACT] Open DevTools → Elements and inspect all elements on the page [ASSERT] Verify product images have descriptive alt attributes reflecting product name/context [ASSERT] Verify decorative images have alt="" [ASSERT] Verify no elements are missing the alt attribute entirely
Assertions: [DOM] Product images have descriptive alt text [DOM] Decorative images have alt="" [DOM] Zero elements with missing (absent) alt attribute
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe image-alt violations
Failure_Signals: product image missing alt; decorative image has descriptive alt (causes unnecessary screen reader verbosity); alt attribute entirely absent

Title: Form Labels - Input Association
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Form with inputs (checkout, registration, or search)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a form on {{FRONT_URL}} (e.g., checkout address form)
[ACT] Open DevTools → Elements panel
[ASSERT] Verify each <input> has an associated <label> via matching for="id"
[ASSERT] Verify aria-label or aria-labelledby is present where a visible label is absent
[ACT] Test with screen reader: Tab to each field and confirm label is announced
Assertions:
[DOM] All inputs have associated labels (for/id pairing or aria-labelledby)
[STATE] Screen reader announces correct label on field focus
[DOM] No orphaned inputs without accessible names
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe label-missing violations
Failure_Signals: input has no associated label; for/id mismatch; aria-labelledby references missing id

Title: Error Identification
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Form with client-side validation
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a form on {{FRONT_URL}} (e.g., checkout address or login)
[ACT] Submit the form with required fields left empty to trigger validation errors
[ASSERT] Verify each error is clearly identified with text (not only by color)
[ASSERT] Verify error messages are programmatically linked to their fields via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage
[ASSERT] Verify error messages provide actionable suggestions where appropriate
Assertions:
[STATE] Errors identified with text label — not color alone
[DOM] Error messages linked to fields via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage
[STATE] Actionable error suggestions provided where feasible (e.g., "Enter a valid email address")
[STATE] Focus moves to first error or errors announced via live region
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe form-error violations
Failure_Signals: error identified by color only; error message not linked to field; no suggestion text; focus not directed to errors after submission

Title: Touch Target Size
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Mobile viewport active (375×812 or 390×844)
Steps:
[NAV] Resize browser to mobile viewport (375px wide) via DevTools device emulation or browser_resize
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} catalog page and cart page
[ACT] Inspect interactive elements: buttons, links, quantity controls, close/dismiss icons
[ASSERT] Verify each touch target measures at least 44×44px via DevTools computed styles
[ASSERT] Verify adequate spacing between adjacent touch targets to prevent mis-taps
Assertions:
[DOM] All interactive touch targets ≥ 44×44px (width and height in computed styles)
[STATE] Adjacent targets have sufficient spacing — no targets overlapping or directly adjacent with less than 8px gap
[STATE] Elements easy to tap accurately without activating neighbouring elements
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no layout errors at mobile viewport
Failure_Signals: touch target measures below 44×44px; targets too close causing mis-taps; overflow or clipping at mobile viewport

Title: VcVariantPicker - States
Preconditions: - Product page with variant picker (VcVariantPicker component) loaded
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}} that has variant options (size, color, etc.)
[ACT] browser_snapshot to inspect VcVariantPicker DOM at rest (default state)
[ASSERT] Verify unselected options are visible and distinguishable
[ACT] browser_hover over an option to trigger hover state
[ASSERT] Inspect hover state visual and ARIA indicators via snapshot
[KEY] Tab to the picker to trigger focus state
[ASSERT] Verify focus state appearance and ARIA focus attributes
[ACT] browser_click to select a variant option
[ASSERT] Inspect selected state: visual highlight + aria-checked or aria-selected = "true"
Assertions:
[DOM] Default, hover, focus, and selected states are visually distinguishable
[DOM] Selected state reflected programmatically: aria-checked="true" or aria-selected="true" on the chosen option
[STATE] Screen reader can distinguish selected from unselected options
[DOM] Each option has an accessible name matching the variant label
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no ARIA state errors on picker interaction
Failure_Signals: states not visually distinguishable; selected state not reflected in aria-checked/aria-selected; accessible name missing on option

Title: Address Popup Column Sort - aria-sort Ascending Transition
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- User has at least 2 saved addresses
- Address selection popup is accessible (checkout shipping step or ship-to widget)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to checkout shipping step on {{FRONT_URL}} and open the address selection popup
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot to capture initial state of the address table header row
[ASSERT] Verify no column header has aria-sort set to "ascending" or "descending" initially (all headers have aria-sort="none" or attribute absent)
[ACT] browser_click on the "Name" (or first sortable) column header
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot to capture post-click header state
[ASSERT] Inspect the clicked column header element: confirm aria-sort="ascending"
[ASSERT] Verify cursor style on the header is "pointer" (via computed styles or DevTools)
[ASSERT] Verify all other column headers have aria-sort="none" or no aria-sort attribute
Assertions:
[DOM] aria-sort="ascending" is present on the clicked column header after first click
[DOM] All other column headers do NOT have aria-sort="ascending" or "descending" — only one active sort indicator at a time
[STATE] Column header cursor is "pointer" on hover, indicating interactivity
[STATE] WCAG 4.1.2: The sort state change is programmatically determinable
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe aria-sort violations after sort interaction
Failure_Signals: aria-sort attribute remains unchanged after click (not wired — VCST-4992 regression); multiple headers show aria-sort="ascending" simultaneously; cursor remains "default" on header hover; aria-sort absent on all headers (attribute not rendered)
Cleanup: Close address popup; no address changes were made

Title: Address Popup Column Sort - aria-sort Descending Transition
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- User has at least 2 saved addresses
- Address selection popup is accessible (checkout shipping step or ship-to widget)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to checkout shipping step on {{FRONT_URL}} and open the address selection popup
[ACT] browser_click on a sortable column header (e.g., "Name") to sort ascending
[ASSERT] Verify aria-sort="ascending" is set on that header
[ACT] browser_click the same column header a second time to reverse sort direction
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot to capture post-second-click header state
[ASSERT] Inspect the same column header element: confirm aria-sort="descending"
[ASSERT] Verify all other column headers still have aria-sort="none" or no aria-sort
Assertions:
[DOM] aria-sort="descending" is set on the column header after second click
[DOM] aria-sort transitions: none → ascending → descending (correct ARIA 1.2 cycle)
[DOM] Only one column header has an active aria-sort value at any point
[STATE] Sort direction visually reflected in the table row order (rows reorder accordingly)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe aria-sort violations; [NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on address re-fetch after sort
Failure_Signals: aria-sort stays "ascending" after second click (toggle not implemented); aria-sort flips to "none" instead of "descending"; table rows do not reorder on second click; multiple headers become active simultaneously
Cleanup: Close address popup; no address changes were made
References: VCST-4992; WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value); ARIA 1.2 §6.27 aria-sort

Title: Address Popup Column Sort - Unsorted Columns aria-sort=none
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- User has at least 2 saved addresses
- Address selection popup is open at checkout shipping step or ship-to widget
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to checkout shipping step on {{FRONT_URL}} and open the address selection popup
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot to capture the address table at default/unsorted state
[ACT] browser_click on one column header to activate a sort on that column
[ASSERT] Verify that the CLICKED column header now has aria-sort="ascending" or "descending"
[ASSERT] Inspect ALL OTHER sortable column headers in the same table: verify each has aria-sort="none"
[ASSERT] Verify non-sortable headers (if any) have no aria-sort attribute at all
Assertions:
[DOM] Only the active sort column has aria-sort set to "ascending" or "descending"
[DOM] All non-active sortable columns have aria-sort="none" explicitly
[DOM] Non-sortable headers do not carry an aria-sort attribute
[STATE] WCAG 4.1.2 compliance: each column sort state uniquely and correctly conveyed to assistive technologies
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no axe aria-allowed-attr violations on column headers
Failure_Signals: multiple headers simultaneously show aria-sort="ascending" (single-active-indicator rule broken); unsorted columns have no aria-sort attribute when they should have aria-sort="none"; aria-sort present on non-sortable header
Cleanup: Close address popup; no address changes were made
References: VCST-4992; WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value); ARIA 1.2 §6.27 aria-sort

Title: Coupon Sidebar — Invalid-Coupon Error Element Has role="alert" (WCAG 3.3.1 / 4.1.3)
Section: Accessibility > Cart > Coupon Sidebar > Error Announcement
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- At least 1 product added to cart.
- Coffee theme active (only Coffee theme is a11y-compliant per feedback_a11y_coffee_only).
- Cart page at {{FRONT_URL}}/cart loaded.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded with coupon sidebar visible
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot — capture initial coupon sidebar state
[ACT] fill the custom coupon code input with an invalid code: 'BOGUS-SR-TEST'
[WAIT] input reflects the typed text
[ACT] click the 'Apply' button (custom coupon card — the arrow/send button on the custom-code input card)
[WAIT] coupon sidebar updates with error state — error message visible below input
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot — capture error state
[ASSERT] evaluate_script: query the DOM for the coupon-card error paragraph — verify role attribute
Assertions:
[DOM] coupon error message element is visible with text 'This code is not valid' or 'Something went wrong with the coupon. Please try again.' [DOM] the error

element has attribute role="alert" — asserting the fix from PR #2303: screen-reader users receive an audible announcement when the error appears (WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, Level AA) [DOM] the error element is not hidden by CSS (display is not none; visibility is not hidden) [STATE] error message text is non-empty — SR has meaningful content to announce
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] zero JS errors during coupon apply attempt; specifically no UnhandledPromiseRejection
[NETWORK] addCoupon mutation response received (HTTP 200); errors[] is empty; isAppliedSuccessfully=false
Failure_Signals:
role attribute absent from error

(VCST-5018 regression — SR silent on error); error

not rendered after invalid code (v-if gate failure); role is set to a non-live value (e.g. role="paragraph") instead of role="alert" or aria-live="assertive"; console TypeError during apply
Cleanup: none — invalid coupon was not applied

Title: Coupon Sidebar — Apply and Trash Buttons Have Descriptive aria-label (WCAG 4.1.2)
Section: Accessibility > Cart > Coupon Sidebar > Button Names
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- At least 1 product added to cart.
- Coffee theme active.
- Cart page at {{FRONT_URL}}/cart loaded with at least 1 preset coupon card visible in the coupon sidebar.
- A valid coupon exists: @td(COUPONS.percentageCode).
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded with coupon sidebar visible and at least 1 preset coupon card rendered
[ASSERT] browser_snapshot — capture sidebar with preset cards
[ASSERT] evaluate_script: query all buttons inside the coupon sidebar widget — for each button, collect its aria-label attribute value
[ASSERT] verify every preset card apply button has aria-label matching pattern 'Apply coupon <code>' where <code> is the coupon code shown on the card (PR #2303 fix)
[ACT] fill the custom coupon code input with {{couponCode}}
[WAIT] input reflects the typed text
[ASSERT] evaluate_script: inspect the custom apply button aria-label — it must contain 'Apply coupon' and the typed code (live interpolation)
[ACT] click the apply button on any preset coupon card
[WAIT] coupon applied — sidebar updates to applied-state with trash/remove icon visible
[ASSERT] evaluate_script: inspect the trash/remove icon button aria-label — must contain 'Remove coupon'
Assertions:
[DOM] every preset coupon card apply button: aria-label is non-empty AND contains 'Apply coupon' followed by the coupon code (VCST-5017 fix — PR #2303)
[DOM] custom coupon input apply button: aria-label is non-empty AND contains 'Apply coupon' text
[DOM] applied-state trash/remove button: aria-label is non-empty AND contains 'Remove coupon' (VCST-5017 fix — PR #2303)
